one_line_ask: Take Vitalé Labs' raw single-take wellness talking-head, clean the noisy on-camera speech, auto-cut it into a punchy highlight sizzle at a target duration, then export platform-ready 9:16 Reel, 9:16 Short and 1:1 feed versions plus a caption/transcript note.
brief: Vitalé Labs is a direct-to-consumer wellness and DNA-supplement brand that publishes short educational clips explaining how personalized nutrition works. We just filmed our lead nutrition scientist, Dr. Nadia Reuter, doing a single continuous take (about 3.5 minutes, shot 3840x2160 landscape on a mirrorless camera) explaining our new "GenoBalance" DNA supplement panel. It is a rough take: she rambles, restarts a couple of sentences, there are long pauses while she checks her notes, and the room has an audible HVAC hum plus a little echo because we shot in an untreated office. We need this turned into ONE tight, premium, trustworthy sizzle Reel — clean and modern and educational, NOT flashy or meme-style. Deliverables and specs: (1) clean up the spoken audio so her voice is clear and the room hum/echo is gone; (2) cut the rambling take down to a punchy ~35-45 second highlight version that keeps the strongest, most watchable moments and drops the dead air — keep viewers glued from the first second; (3) from that finished cut, export three platform-ready versions at the correct dimensions with no stretching: a 9:16 vertical Instagram Reel (1080x1920), a 9:16 vertical YouTube Short (10
